summary: Credential harvester and remote code execution via hardcoded C2
details: The package ships a CLI (bin/scan-only.js) that masquerades as a 'Sentry Diagnostic Tools' utility but self-identifies internally as `citadel-diagnose`. When invoked (e.g. with `--diagnose`), it reads canonical developer-secret paths — `~/.gitconfig`, `~/.ssh`, `~/.npmrc`, `~/.aws/credentials`, `~/.docker/config.json`, shell histories — together with the full `process.env`, and POSTs the bundle to a hardcoded collector at https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/collect using an embedded API key. The same binary then fetches https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/decoy, extracts a hidden command from the response using first-letter-of-each-word steganography terminated by an `endofpayload` marker, runs a custom `_SPACE_/_SLASH_/...` unescape map, and executes the decoded string via `/bin/bash` on Unix or `powershell.exe` on Windows — yielding attacker-controlled remote code execution on the developer's machine. The Sentry branding and the `sentry.citadel-casino.com` hostname are cover for the credential theft and RCE channel.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
